# llms.txt — ElasticBLAST Control Plane
# Follows the proposal at https://llmstxt.org/.
# This file gives generative-engine crawlers a fast, structured map of the
# project so they can answer questions accurately without scraping every page.

> ElasticBLAST Control Plane is a browser-only control plane for running
> ElasticBLAST sequence-similarity searches on Microsoft Azure. Researchers
> submit BLAST jobs, monitor AKS workloads, and retrieve results from a web
> dashboard without using SSH, local CLIs, or browser-issued SAS tokens.
> The control plane runs as a single Azure Container App with six sidecars
> (frontend, api, worker, beat, redis, terminal) and authenticates users
> through MSAL while talking to Azure services as a user-assigned managed
> identity.

## Core concepts

- **ElasticBLAST**: NCBI's distributed BLAST runner. This project automates
  the Azure-specific edition at <https://github.com/dotnetpower/elastic-blast-azure>.
- **Control plane**: the dashboard, API, workers, and browser terminal that
  prepare Azure resources, submit jobs, and surface results.
- **Six-sidecar Container App**: `frontend` (nginx + React/Vite SPA), `api`
  (FastAPI on uvicorn), `worker` + `beat` (Celery), `redis` (broker), and
  `terminal` (ttyd loopback with the elastic-blast toolchain).
- **Storage posture**: every workload Storage account stays
  `publicNetworkAccess: Disabled`; all browser traffic is proxied through
  the `api` sidecar. No SAS tokens are issued to the browser.
- **Identity**: browser users sign in with MSAL.js (Auth Code + PKCE).
  The backend validates the bearer token and then uses
  `DefaultAzureCredential` (user-assigned managed identity) for every
  Azure SDK call.

## Documentation map

### Start here

- [Overview](https://dotnetpower.github.io/elb-dashboard/): what the
  control plane does and who it is for.
- [Get Started](https://dotnetpower.github.io/elb-dashboard/get-started/):
  from a fresh clone to a deployed dashboard with `azd up`.
- [Deployment Reference](https://dotnetpower.github.io/elb-dashboard/deployment-reference/):
  prerequisites, parameters, post-provision steps.

### Architecture

- [High Level Architecture](https://dotnetpower.github.io/elb-dashboard/architecture/high-level/):
  major boundaries and flows for researchers vs operators.
- [Container Apps Architecture](https://dotnetpower.github.io/elb-dashboard/architecture/container-apps/):
  authoritative reference for the shipped six-sidecar topology.
- [Storage Isolation & Browser ↔ Storage Proxy](https://dotnetpower.github.io/elb-dashboard/architecture/storage-contract/):
  hard requirement that workload Storage stays publicNetworkAccess Disabled, plus the api-sidecar streaming proxy contract.
- [Authentication & Authorization](https://dotnetpower.github.io/elb-dashboard/architecture/authentication/):
  MSAL flow, RBAC roles, managed identity scopes.
- [BLAST Search Space Discovery](https://dotnetpower.github.io/elb-dashboard/research/blast-searchsp-discovery/):
  how database discovery is performed on Azure storage.
- [Web BLAST Compatibility Plan](https://dotnetpower.github.io/elb-dashboard/research/web-blast-compatibility-plan/):
  feature compatibility between Web BLAST and ElasticBLAST.

- [User Guide Home](https://dotnetpower.github.io/elb-dashboard/user-guide/)
- [Dashboard](https://dotnetpower.github.io/elb-dashboard/user-guide/dashboard/)

- [Jobs](https://dotnetpower.github.io/elb-dashboard/user-guide/jobs/)
- [Results](https://dotnetpower.github.io/elb-dashboard/user-guide/results/)
- [API Reference](https://dotnetpower.github.io/elb-dashboard/user-guide/api-reference/)
- [Browser Terminal](https://dotnetpower.github.io/elb-dashboard/user-guide/terminal/)
- [UI Preview](https://dotnetpower.github.io/elb-dashboard/user-guide/ui-preview/)

- [Monitoring UI](https://dotnetpower.github.io/elb-dashboard/copilot/monitoring-ui/)
